articles.title: Kalshi Wants Your ID Whether You Gamble or Not (You Know, for Kids)
articles.summary: Kalshi is apparently collecting identification from users regardless of whether they actually place bets, suggesting the platform is casting a wide net for user verification and data collection. This signals either aggressive compliance ahead of regulatory scrutiny or a broader strategy to build user profiles before monetizing non-gambling features, both of which could reshape how prediction markets handle customer onboarding.

articles.title: Kalshi's not having a good time in Ohio
articles.summary: Kalshi is facing regulatory pushback or legal troubles in Ohio that's disrupting their operations in the state. This signals growing scrutiny of prediction markets at the state level, which could create a patchwork of regulations that makes expansion harder for platforms trying to go mainstream.

articles.title: The investors profiting from prediction markets? The top 1%, of course
articles.summary: A new analysis reveals that the top 1% of prediction market traders are capturing the lion's share of profits, mirroring wealth concentration patterns across traditional finance. This concentration could undermine the democratic promise of prediction markets while creating liquidity concerns as casual bettors get squeezed out by sophisticated players.

articles.title: Kalshi Expands Commodity Predictions Market Amid War Volatility
articles.summary: Kalshi is launching new commodity prediction markets as geopolitical tensions send oil, wheat, and other raw material prices into wild swings. Smart move, since nothing drives betting volume quite like uncertainty, and war-fueled commodity chaos creates the perfect storm of volatility that traders love to bet on.

articles.title: Prediction markets are breaking the news and becoming their own beat
articles.summary: Prediction markets are increasingly becoming the first place major news breaks, with traders moving odds before traditional media even catches wind of developing stories. This shift turns prediction platforms into must-watch news sources themselves, giving sharp bettors who monitor market movements a serious information edge over everyone else.

articles.title: Kalshi Survivor 50 Episode 8 Predictions - Who Goes Home on Survivor Tonight?
articles.summary: Kalshi is running prediction markets on who gets voted off Survivor tonight in Episode 8 of Season 50. Reality TV betting markets are exploding as platforms discover that devoted fan bases will trade on literally anything they can obsess over.

articles.title: Prediction markets are a gamble we can't afford | Editorial
articles.summary: Some editorial board thinks prediction markets are too risky and we should avoid them entirely. Classic regulatory fear-mongering that could fuel more government crackdowns on platforms where people actually put money behind their predictions.

articles.title: Ohio slaps Kalshi with $5 million fine for unlicensed sports betting
articles.summary: Ohio regulators hit Kalshi with a $5 million fine for allegedly operating unlicensed sports betting markets in the state. The penalty signals that prediction market platforms can't hide behind CFTC approval when states decide their event contracts look too much like traditional sports bets.

articles.title: Are Prediction Markets Gambling?
articles.summary: The age-old question of whether prediction markets constitute gambling is heating up again as regulators scrutinize platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket. The answer could determine whether these markets get regulated into oblivion or finally gain mainstream acceptance as legitimate forecasting tools.

articles.title: Kalshi, Polymarket lobby as insider trading, betting eyed by Congress
articles.summary: Congress is scrutinizing prediction markets for potential insider trading issues while Kalshi and Polymarket ramp up their lobbying efforts on Capitol Hill. The regulatory spotlight threatens to reshape how these platforms operate, potentially limiting the types of markets they can offer or imposing stricter oversight that could dampen the rapid growth both companies have enjoyed.

articles.title: Prediction market Kalshi sues Montana attorney general to prevent gambling enforcement
articles.summary: Kalshi filed a lawsuit against Montana's attorney general to block enforcement actions that would classify prediction markets as illegal gambling. The case could set crucial precedent for how states regulate prediction markets, potentially determining whether platforms can operate freely or face a patchwork of state-by-state gambling restrictions.

articles.title: Prediction markets will grow to $1 trillion by 2030, Bernstein estimates
articles.summary: Bernstein analysts predict the prediction markets industry will balloon to $1 trillion by 2030, up from today's relatively tiny market size. If they're right, we're looking at the mainstreaming of prediction markets from a niche betting curiosity into a legitimate financial asset class that could rival traditional derivatives markets.
